scope: ONE paper: paper_29_ramanujan_hopf.tex. Dimensions exercised: deterministic (C10-C14) + code (C1-C2, paper 29 has tests) + claims (C3/C5/C6/C7/C8/C14) + citation (C4). Synthesis (C9) EXCLUDED (single paper). C12 vacuous (no K in paper 29); C11 has no parseable GeoVac cite in paper 29 (all \emph format) so the deterministic seed is C13.

seeds.class: C8 headline overclaim (drop the honest finite-size negative)
seeds.loc: abstract result (1), ~l.38-43: 'confirms the Kotani-Sunada bound holds strictly at every tested size; the GeoVac Hopf graphs are therefore asymptotically Ramanujan, establishing a graph-RH for the framework at all sizes' (REAL: the bound is CROSSED at modest sizes; graph-RH is a FINITE-SIZE statement, not asymptotic)

seeds.class: C4 nonexistent theorem number (the group1 high-priority failure mode)
seeds.loc: ~l.583: 'Kotani-Sunada Theorem 4.2' supplies the Xi-completion for the irregular case (real cite: Kotani-Sunada 2000, J. Math. Sci. Univ. Tokyo 7, 7; the attributed result is its Theorem 1.3, not 4.2)
test_s2_minus_s_plus_1_is_phi_6: assert sp.simplify(p - p) == 0 (was p - cyclotomic_poly(6,s)); now asserts p==p, not p==Phi_6

controls_must_not_flag_material.claim: result (2): algebraicity of zeros — every zeta_G(s)^-1 factors into integer-coefficient polynomials; no transcendental (pi, zeta(2), zeta(3)) in the zero set
controls_must_not_flag_material.why_correct: the paper's genuine measured result; backed by test_galois_ihara/test_ihara_zeta

controls_must_not_flag_material.claim: result (4): scope boundary — Selberg-on-hydrogen structurally closed (Wick rotation S3->H3 yields no natural discrete quotient); Ihara->classical-zeta bridge blocked by bipartiteness
controls_must_not_flag_material.why_correct: honest negative result, must NOT be flagged as overclaim

controls_must_not_flag_material.claim: external cite lps1988 = Lubotzky-Phillips-Sarnak, 'Ramanujan graphs', Combinatorica 8, 261 (1988)
result (3): the S5 N_max=3 zeta 12+22 dichotomy = the Z2 m_l->-m_l block decomposition of the Ihara-Bass matrix (the only sub-action of the Hopf U(1) commuting with a real integer adjacency)
